summary: Excellent audience‑sourced recording of a stellar Ventura show, highlighted by a blazing second set with Morning Dew, Dancing in the Street, Midnight Hour and a rare Brent cover.
review: The four five‑star reviews rave about this 1984 Ventura performance, calling it possibly the best of the venue’s shows and singling out the second set’s percussion/improvisation segue into Morning Dew, plus standout numbers like Dancing in the Street, Midnight Hour and a one‑off Brent rendition of I Just Want to Make Love to You. The recording sounds front‑and‑center with great clarity, though a slight bass boost would improve it further.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviewers gave 5 stars and praised both the musical performance (especially the second set) and the audio quality; minor bass‑boost suggestion keeps the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, front‑and‑center sound that captures the band well; reviewers note a slight need for a bass bump but overall very listenable.

standout_songs: I Just Want to Make Love to You
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set praised for seamless percussion → improvisation → Morning Dew transition and overall high energy; Garcia never left the stage between Not Fade Away and Brokedown Palace, adding to the flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s growl and guitar tone are highlighted; he stayed onstage through the Not Fade Away → Brokedown Palace transition.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent delivered a rare cover of 'I Just Want to Make Love to You', noted as a special moment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Percussion and improvisation segment received strong praise, indicating standout drumming.
